response: Originating from 1960s U.S. Defense Department research and the 1969 ARPANET demonstration, the internet evolved through foundational milestones like the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. After weathering the late-1990s dot-com boom and crash, the platform expanded rapidly with the rise of social media, cloud computing, and mobile access following the 2007 iPhone launch. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and has transformed from a specialized research network into an indispensable global infrastructure that underpins nearly every aspect of modern commerce, communication, and entertainment.
